title: Consultation: Proposed Residential Indoor Air Quality Guidelines for Benzene - Canada.ca
content:
## Consultation: Proposed Residential Indoor Air Quality Guidelines for Benzene

## Current status: Closed
Opened on September 30, 2023 and closed to new input on November 29, 2023.
Health Canada wants your feedback on the proposed Residential Indoor Air
Quality Guidelines (RIAQG) for Benzene.

## Join in: how to participate
- Read the consultation document: Proposed Residential Indoor
    Air Quality Guidelines for Benzene
- Submit your comments to the Water and Air Quality Bureau, Health
 Canada. Please include the title of the consultation document. You can
 provide your comments in either of 2 ways:
Send an email with your comments to air@hc-sc.gc.ca .
Send a letter with your comments to the address in contact information . We must receive all comments by November 29, 2023.

## Who is the focus of this consultation
We want to hear from scientific or technical audiences, including:
- industry stakeholders
- academic communities
- health protection agencies
We also welcome comments from members of the public.

## Key items for discussion
We're seeking your feedback on the proposed guidelines for benzene.
The RIAQG reviews the epidemiological, toxicological and exposure research
 on benzene as well as the conclusions from a number of comprehensive
 reviews from internationally recognized health and environmental
organizations. The intent is to:
- recommend various ways to reduce exposure to benzene in indoor
    environments
- propose a long-term exposure limits for benzene in residential
 indoor air, which would minimize risks to human health
Water and Air Quality Bureau, Health Canada 3rd Floor, 269 Laurier Avenue Wes Address Locator 4903B Ottawa, ON K1A 0K9 Email: air@hc-sc.gc.ca
